title: Dryopteris lanciloba
intro: Dryopteris lanciloba, also known as the lance-lobed wood fern, is a species of fern in the Dryopteridaceae family, characterized by its distinctive lance-shaped leaf lobes. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may help to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris lanciloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Dryopteris lanciloba include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Dryopteris lanciloba is a species of the genus Dryopteris, which belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the wood fern family.
